2007-07-01.title: Israel Resumes Transfer of Taxes to Palestinians
2007-07-01.abstract: The move ends Israel’s fiscal isolation of the Palestinians, which began with the installation of a Hamas government last year.

2007-07-01.title: Officials Say Civilian Casualties in Iraq Declined
2007-07-01.abstract: American and Iraqi officials said that they were seeing a decline in the monthly civilian casualty count in June.

2007-07-01.title: 4 Held in Scottish Attack as British See Broader Plot
2007-07-01.abstract: Britain raised its terrorism threat alert to its highest level after two men crashed an S.U.V. into doors at Glasgow Airport and turned the vehicle into a fireball.

2007-07-01.title: Pope Urges Reconciliation for China’s Catholics
2007-07-01.abstract: In a letter, Pope Benedict acknowledged suffering by Catholics under Communist rule, but said it was time to forgive past wrongdoings.

2007-07-01.title: More Afghan Civilians Killed in Airstrikes
2007-07-01.abstract: Local Afghan officials reported that dozens of civilians, and perhaps a great many more, were killed during United States-led coalition airstrikes.

2007-07-02.title: U.S. Ties Iran to Deadly Iraq Attack
2007-07-02.abstract: Iranian operatives helped in a January raid in which five American soldiers were killed, a U.S. military spokesman said today.

2007-07-02.title: No Oil Yet, but African Isle Finds Slippery Dealings
2007-07-02.abstract: The tiny island nation of São Tomé may have attracted oil-related corruption without producing any oil.

2007-07-02.title: In Hunt for Bomb Plotters, Britain Sees a Qaeda Link
2007-07-02.abstract: The police expanded their search for the plotters of the attempted attacks, arresting a fifth suspect. At least one of the detainees was said to be a physician.

2007-07-02.title: Hamas Makes Arrests in BBC Reporter’s Case
2007-07-02.abstract: A spokesman for the group that claims to have kidnapped the BBC’s Gaza reporter was arrested, as were two others.

2007-07-02.title: Fascist Overtones From Blithely Oblivious Rock Fans
2007-07-02.abstract: During a Croatian rock star’s shows, some young fans give Nazi salutes and wear the black caps of the Nazi puppet regime that ruled the country during World War II.

2007-07-03.title: Argentina’s President Steps Aside to Support Wife as His Successor
2007-07-03.abstract: If Senator Cristina Fernández de Kirchner wins the presidency, Néstor Kirchner could return to office in the future.

2007-07-03.title: Exiled Tycoon Says Russia Claims That He Is Inciting Coup
2007-07-03.abstract: Boris A. Berezovsky, one of President Vladimir V. Putin’s fiercest critics, claimed to be facing charges of calling for the violent overthrow of the Russian government.

2007-07-03.title: Palestinian Civil Servants to Be Paid
2007-07-03.abstract: Workers loyal to the new emergency government will be paid; employees who remain allied with Hamas will not.

2007-07-03.title: Russia: Divers Scour for $200,000 Watch
2007-07-03.abstract: A diamond-studded wristwatch said to be worth $200,000 became the subject of an intensive search after a visitor said he dropped it into the Neva River in St. Petersburg.

2007-07-03.title: This Year at Marienbad, They’re Still Taking the Waters
2007-07-03.abstract: In the quiet spa town now known as Marianske Lazne, visitors can relax in the footsteps of Czar Nicholas II,  Friedrich Nietzsche, Franz Kafka and Rudyard Kipling.

2007-07-04.title: Brown’s Reaction to Terrorist Threat, So Different From Blair’s, Reassures Many
2007-07-04.abstract: For the many Britons who had expressed fatigue with Tony Blair’s apocalyptic view of terrorism, Gordon Brown’s very dourness was a welcome change.

2007-07-04.title: Pakistan’s Battles Against Islamic Militants Reach the Capital
2007-07-04.abstract: A months-long standoff, between Pakistani authorities and militant Islamic students, erupted in fierce clashes that left at least nine people dead and scores wounded.

2007-07-04.title: BBC Journalist Freed in Gaza
2007-07-04.abstract: Alan Johnston, the BBC correspondent who was kidnapped in March, is in Israel, preparing to fly home to Britain.

2007-07-04.title: Estonia: Soviet Soldiers Are Reburied
2007-07-04.abstract: Estonia reburied the remains of eight Soviet soldiers, which had been exhumed from a memorial site in April over Russian objections, setting off clashes.

2007-07-04.title: Leader in Mosque Siege is Captured
2007-07-04.abstract: As Pakistani forces tightened a cordon around the mosque in Islamabad Maulana Mohammad Abdul Aziz was caught trying to slip out dressed as a woman, officials said.

2007-07-05.title: No Fast Gain for Hamas After Release of Journalist
2007-07-05.abstract: The role of Hamas in securing the release of Alan Johnston is not enough to warrant any immediate change in policy toward it, Western and Israeli officials said Wednesday.

2007-07-05.title: China Reportedly Urged Omitting Pollution-Death Estimates
2007-07-05.abstract: Chinese government officials pressed the World Bank into removing estimates of the number of premature deaths linked to pollution in China from a bank report.

2007-07-05.title: Doctor Accused in Glasgow Attack Described as Loner Angry About the Iraq War
2007-07-05.abstract: Associates of Bilal Abdulla say he was a religious zealot and a lone wolf whose anger about political developments in Iraq may have driven him to an act of terrorism.

2007-07-05.title: Rwanda: Army Major Guilty in U.N. Killings
2007-07-05.abstract: Bernard Ntuyahaga, a former major in the Rwandan Army, was found guilty in a Belgian court of killing 10 United Nations peacekeepers from Belgium at the start of the Rwandan genocide in 1994.

2007-07-05.title: British Police Work to Piece Together Plot’s Details
2007-07-05.abstract: As police today worked to untangle the connections among the suspects in last week’s failed car bombings, four other Muslims were convicted of terrorist offenses in two trials.

2007-07-06.title: London Bombers Sped to Glasgow, Authorities Say
2007-07-06.abstract: A week after the intended bombings, evidence is emerging that two doctors were the main operatives, if not leaders, of a network of other medical professionals.

2007-07-06.title: In India, a Prayer for Rain Despite a Deluge
2007-07-06.abstract: Rains in India can destroy lives and livelihoods and send ripples through the economy.

2007-07-06.title: Ireland: Children Rescued at Sea
2007-07-06.abstract: The Coast Guard rescued more than 110 participants in a sailing regatta, most of them children 11 to 15 years old, from Dublin Bay after a sudden squall capsized a number of the small craft. All had life jackets. Fifteen were taken to hospitals with symptoms of hypothermia. Others described on television how they were thrown into the water off Dun Laoghaire when the wind toppled the boats.

2007-07-06.title: Russia: No Extradition for Poison Suspect
2007-07-06.abstract: The prosecutor general formally notified British prosecutors that Russia would not extradite the former K.G.B. officer Andrei K. Lugovoi, left, to stand trial on the murder charge he faces in Britain, Interfax reported. Mr. Lugovoi is accused in the death of Alexander V. Litvinenko, a former Russian intelligence officer turned Kremlin critic who was fatally poisoned by polonium 210, a rare radioactive isotope, last year. Russia has said its Constitution bars handing Russian citizens over to other nations and has offered to consider prosecuting Mr. Lugovoi in Russia.

2007-07-06.title: British Police Charge Suspect in Car Bombings
2007-07-06.abstract: Also today, the F.B.I. said two other medical doctors arrested in connection with the bungled attacks had inquired about practicing medicine in the U.S.

2007-07-07.title: Iraq Hangs Insurgent Who Killed Shiite Leader in Bombing of Shrine in 2003
2007-07-07.abstract: A suspected member of Al Qaeda in Mesopotamia was executed for an August 2003 bomb attack, which killed Ayatollah Muhammad Bakr al-Hakim, a politician and cleric.

2007-07-07.title: A U.S. Diplomat With an Extraordinary Global View
2007-07-07.abstract: America’s first blind diplomat, Avraham Rabby, spent the last two years surveying Trinidad’s political landscape and composing cables to Washington in Braille.

2007-07-07.title: Shamanism Enjoys Revival in Techno-Savvy South Korea
2007-07-07.abstract: There are an estimated 300 shamanistic temples within an hour of Seoul’s bustling city center, and in them, shamans perform their clamorous ceremonies every day.

2007-07-07.title: In Break From History and Scandal, Canada Chooses a Civilian to Lead the Mounties
2007-07-07.abstract: The new chief, William J. S. Elliot, a lawyer and public servant, made little attempt to diminish the problems facing the national police force.

2007-07-07.title: Musharraf’s Plane Fired Upon as Mosque Standoff Continues
2007-07-07.abstract: Security officials said they recovered two antiaircraft guns and one submachine gun with a telescopic sight from a rooftop barely a mile from the airport in Rawalpindi, Pakistan.

2007-07-08.title: British Identify Two ‘Principal’ Suspects
2007-07-08.abstract: The two principal suspects are almost certainly the two men arrested after crashing their Jeep into the Glasgow airport.

2007-07-08.title: Iraq Attack Death Toll, at 150, May Be War’s Worst
2007-07-08.abstract: The attack yesterday in a poor Shiite Turkmen town was one of the deadliest single bombings since the 2003 invasion of Iraq.

2007-07-08.title: Youth Groups Created by Kremlin Serve Putin’s Cause
2007-07-08.abstract: A youth movement seeks the ideological cultivation, some say indoctrination, of the first generation to come of age in post-Soviet Russia.

2007-07-08.title: Pakistani President Tells Militants: Surrender or Die
2007-07-08.abstract: General Pervez Musharraf, in his first comments on the five-day siege of militants, said the students “have defamed Islam” and “embarrassed Pakistan.”

2007-07-08.title: Pledging Allegiance to the Earth, in New Jersey
2007-07-08.abstract: Musicians including Kanye West, Roger Waters and The Police performed at the American site of the Live Earth campaign in Giants Stadium on Saturday.

2007-07-09.title: 4 Guilty in Failed 2005 London Bombing
2007-07-09.abstract: The convictions of four men today illuminated Britain’s continuing battle with Islamic radicalism.

2007-07-09.title: U.S. Diplomat Hints at Delay for Kosovo Independence
2007-07-09.abstract: The assessment is likely to be seen as a setback for Kosovo’s ethnic Albanian leadership.

2007-07-09.title: Iraqi Warns of Grave Results if U.S. Pulls Out
2007-07-09.abstract: The Iraqi foreign minister also asserted that Turkey had massed 140,000 troops near the northern border.

2007-07-09.title: Pakistan Military Storms a Mosque to Free Students After Negotiations Fail to End a Siege
2007-07-09.abstract: The Pakistani military mounted an operation to enter a mosque complex where Islamic militants and students have been holed up since last week, a military spokesman told reporters.

2007-07-09.title: Severe Personality Shift Seen in Bomb Suspect
2007-07-09.abstract: Kafeel Ahmed, described as the driver who crashed into the Glasgow International Airport, went from being a good-natured top student to a radical who spoke of “fighting for Islam.”

2007-07-10.title: Scores Dead in Battle at Pakistani Mosque
2007-07-10.abstract: The battle at the Red Mosque presents a test of Gen. Pervez Musharraf’s ability to root out religious radicals.

2007-07-10.title: Drinkers in Korea Dial for Designated Drivers
2007-07-10.abstract: Tens of thousands of “replacement drivers” in Seoul, South Korea, make their livings by delivering inebriated people and their cars home.

2007-07-10.title: Gleaning History From a Blair Aide’s Diary
2007-07-10.abstract: A 757-page collection of entries by the former British prime minister’s closest adviser, spin doctor, hand-holder and policy enforcer will be published at the end of July.

2007-07-10.title: Russia: Security Council Official Resigns
2007-07-10.abstract: Igor S. Ivanov, a long-serving Russian diplomat, has tendered his resignation as secretary of the National Security Council, a powerful security and foreign policy advisory group in the Russian government. Mr. Ivanov was Russia’s foreign minister from 1998 until 2004; as secretary of the Security Council, he oversaw a policy of toughening Moscow’s position on Iran’s nuclear program, bringing Russia’s stance more in line with that of the United States. His influence was waning recently, Russia’s Vedomosti newspaper suggested in an article that linked his departure to an internal restructuring, rather than any change policy on Iran. It said Mr. Ivanov’s offer to resign may signal Mr. Putin’s intention to boost the importance of the Security Council in Russian politics by putting a more influential figure in the job.

2007-07-10.title: China: Body of Climber Is Found
2007-07-10.abstract: The body of Christine Boskoff, an American mountain climber missing since November, has been found in the remote mountains of Sichuan Province, a climbing group that helped conduct the search said. Ms. Boskoff, 39, was last seen Nov. 12 at a monastery several thousand feet below the summit of Genyen Mountain with her longtime companion and climbing partner, Charlie Fowler. They set out that day to climb the 20,354-foot mountain. Both are believed to have died in an avalanche. Mr. Fowler’s body was found in December but the search for Ms. Boskoff was suspended because of the weather. Both climbers had reached the summit of many of the world’s highest peaks, including Mount Everest. Ms. Boskoff’s body has not been recovered because it was found in a particularly treacherous area, said David C. Jones, a co-owner with Ms. Boskoff of Mountain Madness, a Seattle adventure travel service. Mr. Jones said it could be several months before her body is recovered. He said searchers found a passport, camera and other items with Ms. Boskoff.

2007-07-11.title: World Bank Report on Governing Finds Level Playing Field
2007-07-11.abstract: A World Bank report released Tuesday found that it is not just poor countries that struggle with corruption and flawed government.

2007-07-11.title: China Shuts Down Western-Run Newsletter
2007-07-11.abstract: The decision to shut down the newsletter may reflect China’s growing sensitivity to the role of local and foreign-financed civil organizations.

2007-07-11.title: British Jurors Fail to Reach Verdict for 2 in Botched Transit Attack
2007-07-11.abstract: The trial of six men accused of conspiring to bomb the London transport system in 2005 ended when the jury failed to reach a verdict on two of the defendants and was discharged.

2007-07-11.title: Hamas Boycotts Meeting of Palestinian Legislature
2007-07-11.abstract: There was no quorum, allowing Palestinian President Mahmoud Abbas to extend his emergency cabinet.

2007-07-11.title: 4 Men Sentenced to Life in London Plot
2007-07-11.abstract: Four of the six men accused of conspiring to bomb the transportation system  in 2005 were sentenced today.

2007-07-12.title: In Poland, a Jewish Revival Thrives — Minus Jews
2007-07-12.abstract: In Krakow, a new generation of non-Jewish Poles is rediscovering a culture that was nearly annihilated by the Holocaust.

2007-07-12.title: France: Former Premier May Face Smear Charges
2007-07-12.abstract: The former prime minister Dominique de Villepin may face charges for his part in a 2004 plot to smear Nicolas Sarkozy before Mr. Sarkozy won the presidency. The so-called Clearstream affair involved false documentation that listed Mr. Sarkozy, then Mr. de Villepin’s chief political rival, as the beneficiary of bribes paid to a secret bank account. Mr. de Villepin was called back from a vacation last week when investigators searched his Paris home and office. He has since issued a statement saying that he will answer investigators’ questions later this month and that he may be placed under official investigation.

2007-07-12.title: Chile: Supreme Court Judge Rejects Extradition of Fujimori
2007-07-12.abstract: Rejecting recommendations by government prosecutors and lower courts, a Chilean Supreme Court judge has ruled that Alberto Fujimori, the former president of Peru, should not be extradited to his homeland. Mr. Fujimori was overthrown in 2000 and left exile in Japan to come to Chile in 2005. Peruvian courts have charged him with corruption, human rights violations and other crimes during the decade he was in power, but Judge Orlando Álvarez said they had not supplied sufficient proof. The Justice Minister, María Zavala, said, “We’ve lost the battle but not the war.” She predicted that the verdict would be overturned when an appeal reached the full Supreme Court.

2007-07-12.title: Man Arrested in Spain Planned to Bomb a Building, Official Says
2007-07-12.abstract: Aritz Arginzoniz Zubiarre, who was arrested in Spain this week, was caught carrying a revolver, false documents and two timers for triggering bombs.

2007-07-12.title: $282 Million Stolen in Heist at Private Bank in Baghdad
2007-07-12.abstract: Several officials speculated that the robbers, who were guards at the bank, had connections to the militias, allowing them to escape without being searched.

2007-07-13.title: Australia Cites Links Between Doctor and Bomb-Plot Suspects
2007-07-13.abstract: The police in Australia have established links between an Indian doctor they arrested last week and the suspects in the failed London and Glasgow bombings.

2007-07-13.title: Nigeria: Fourth Child Kidnapped
2007-07-13.abstract: Another toddler was kidnapped by gunmen in the volatile Niger Delta region, the fourth child in less than two months.

2007-07-13.title: Germans Weigh Civil Rights and Public Safety
2007-07-13.abstract: Fearful of terrorism, Germany is edging away from its deep aversion to intrusive and harsh enforcement tactics.

2007-07-13.title: A Firm Bush Tells Congress Not to Dictate War Policy
2007-07-13.abstract: Hours later, the House responded by voting almost totally along party lines to require that the U.S. withdraw most combat troops from Iraq by April 1.

2007-07-13.title: Chechnya: Russia Liable in Disappearance
2007-07-13.abstract: The European Court of Human Rights ordered Russia to pay damages to a Chechen man for the disappearance of his brother, who had been detained by Russian authorities in October 2000. The ruling, which blamed Russia for failing to make a record of the detention of Ayubkhan Magomadov, said that Russia had offered “no plausible explanation as to what happened to him after his detention.” Mr. Magomadov is presumed dead, one of thousands of people thought to have been killed by police and soldiers during the Chechen war that began in 1999.

2007-07-14.title: Russia Steps Back From Key Arms Treaty
2007-07-14.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin announced today that Russia would suspend its obligations under a cornerstone cold war-era arms limitation treaty.

2007-07-14.title: Carving Plight of Coal Miners, He Churns China
2007-07-14.abstract: Zhang Jianhua’s temerity in representing Chinese coal miners through life-size sculpture has earned his work what might be called a soft ban.

2007-07-14.title: United Nations: Hariri Investigation Slows
2007-07-14.abstract: The United Nations investigation of the 2005 killing of the former Lebanese prime minister Rafik Hariri has identified a number of people possibly involved, but Lebanon’s worsening situation has made it difficult to gain cooperation from witnesses and recruit staff members, the inquiry’s chief, Serge Brammertz, said in a report to the Security Council. He said his commission had discovered many “commonalities” between the killing and 17 political attacks.

2007-07-14.title: G.O.P. Senators Press to Change Strategy in Iraq
2007-07-14.abstract: Two senators proposed requiring President Bush to present a plan by October that begins limiting the involvement of U.S. forces.

2007-07-14.title: Peacekeepers Accused of Smuggling Gold
2007-07-14.abstract: United Nations peacekeepers in Congo are under investigation on charges of exchanging food for gold with members of militias from neighboring Rwanda.

2007-07-15.title: Medellín’s Nonconformist Mayor Turns Blight to Beauty
2007-07-15.abstract: Mayor Sergio Fajardo has brought luxurious public buildings to areas once ravaged by cocaine wars.

2007-07-15.title: North Koreans Say They’ve Shut Nuclear Reactor
2007-07-15.abstract: The North Korean claim, synchronized with a shipment of fuel oil from South Korea, marked the first step toward reversing a confrontation with the U.S.

2007-07-15.title: Hamas Declares as Illegal Latest Effort at Government
2007-07-15.abstract: Hamas asked Palestinians not to deal with the new caretaker government a day after it was sworn in by Palestinian president Mahmoud Abbas of Fatah.

2007-07-15.title: North Koreans Say They’ve Shut Nuclear Reactor
2007-07-15.abstract: The North Korean claim, which was synchronized with the arrival of a shipment of fuel oil from South Korea, marked the first step toward reversing a confrontation with the U.S.

2007-07-15.title: British Release 2 Men Detained After Failed Bombings
2007-07-15.abstract: The two men, who have not been identified by name, were arrested at the residences of the Royal Alexandra Hospital, near Glasgow.

2007-07-16.title: Russian Pullback on Arms Treaty Obscures Progress, Official Says
2007-07-16.abstract: Despite Russia’s announcement that it’s withdrawing from a key European arms treaty, a senior U.S. official hopes differences can be resolved.

2007-07-16.title: Japan Learns Dreaded Task of Jury Duty
2007-07-16.abstract: Preparing to adopt jury-style trials in 2009, Japan is holding mock trials to overcome cultural obstacles.

2007-07-16.title: 24 Iranians Escape From Iraqi Prison
2007-07-16.abstract: The Iraqi police said that 24 Iranians who had been detained for entering the country illegally had escaped from a prison on the Iraqi side of the porous border.

2007-07-16.title: Top U.S. Official Asks Congress Not to Put Limits on Iraq Mission
2007-07-16.abstract: Stephen J. Hadley, the national security adviser, said the administration’s troop-increase plan needed time to work.

2007-07-16.title: 24 Iranians, Held for Illegal Entry, Escape From Iraqi Prison
2007-07-16.abstract: Iraqi police managed to capture four of the escapees after imposing a curfew on Badra, where the prison is located.

2007-07-17.title: British to Expel 4 Russian Diplomats
2007-07-17.abstract: The foreign secretary, David Miliband, also said Britain would tighten visa requirements for Russian officials and businesspeople.

2007-07-17.title: Blast Kills 13 in Pakistani Capital
2007-07-17.abstract: A blast went off at a site in Islamabad where the embattled former chief justice of the Supreme Court was set to speak.

2007-07-17.title: Bush to Bolster Abbas and Seek Peace Talks
2007-07-17.abstract: The Bush administration signaled that it will make a major push for peace between Israelis and Palestinians.

2007-07-17.title: Derailed Train Sends Up Toxic Cloud in Ukraine
2007-07-17.abstract: A toxic cloud from the crash poisoned about 20 people and forced another about 800 to evacuate their homes, authorities said.

2007-07-17.title: Attacks in Kirkuk and Diyala Kill More Than 100 Iraqis
2007-07-17.abstract: The Kirkuk attack stoked fears that intensified American military operations in Baghdad may have led insurgents to move their operations to more vulnerable locations.

2007-07-18.title: ‘Key Judgments’ on Terrorist Threat to U.S.
2007-07-18.abstract: Following are the “key judgments” released by the White House from a July 2007 National Intelligence Estimate on “The Terrorist Threat to the U.S. Homeland.”

2007-07-18.title: 16 Pakistani Soldiers Killed in Attack
2007-07-18.abstract: A military convoy was attacked this morning in the lawless tribal region on the border with Afghanistan.

2007-07-18.title: Plane Crashes in Brazil; 176 Feared Dead
2007-07-18.abstract: A plane landing at an airport in São Paulo, Brazil, skidded off a runway and crashed into a gas station.

2007-07-18.title: Metal Cots, Takeout Pizza and a Long Night of Recriminations
2007-07-18.abstract: A rare all-night Senate session unfolded earlier in the day with the traditional pageantry: the unveiling of nearly a dozen metal cots, in case senators wished to rest during debate.

2007-07-18.title: North Korea Offers Nuclear Concessions
2007-07-18.abstract: North Korea today offered to disable all its nuclear weapons programs in return for aid, an envoy said.

2007-07-19.title: Gaza’s Economy, Already Fragile, May Collapse Unless Crossings Are Reopened, U.N. Reports
2007-07-19.abstract: Both Israel and the Fatah leader, President Mahmoud Abbas, have been in no hurry to help Hamas by working to regularize Gaza’s economic life.

2007-07-19.title: Britain: 4 Get Prison in Cartoon Case
2007-07-19.abstract: Four men were sentenced to prison for inciting murder and racial hatred last year during a raucous demonstration against the publication of cartoons of the Prophet Mohammed. Marching among several hundred protesters in front of the Danish Embassy here, the men had called for the death of those responsible for publishing the cartoons, which were first published in a Danish newspaper in 2005. Three of the men  Mizanur Rahman, 24, Umran Javed, 27, and Abdul Muhid 25  were sentenced to six years in prison; the fourth, Abdul Saleem, 32 was sentenced to four years.

2007-07-19.title: Russia Expels 4 British Diplomats
2007-07-19.abstract: The move was a response to Britain’s expulsion of the same number of Russian diplomats earlier this week.

2007-07-19.title: Chirac’s Letter to the French People
2007-07-19.abstract: A letter by former President Jacques Chirac of France published  in Le Monde under the title “Political Funding: What I Want to Tell the French.”

2007-07-19.title: Judge Questions Former French President in Political Funding Case
2007-07-19.abstract: Former President Jacques Chirac of France was questioned about accusations that he had a role in a party funding corruption scandal when he was mayor of Paris.

2007-07-20.title: Prosecutors Seek to Close São Paulo Airport in Wake of Brazil’s Worst Airline Disaster
2007-07-20.abstract: Government prosecutors argued that Congonhas, the country’s busiest airport, is a menace both to passengers and residents.

2007-07-20.title: Bombings in Pakistan Leave at Least 48 Dead
2007-07-20.abstract: As three suicide bombings left at least 48 dead, the government sought to tame the disorder by resuscitating a widely criticized and now collapsed peace deal in Pakistan’s tribal areas.

2007-07-20.title: Spain: Boat Carrying Migrants Capsizes
2007-07-20.abstract: Spanish rescue crews pulled 48 migrants to safety after their wooden boat was spotted struggling in large waves south of Tenerife in the Canary Islands, but 50 others were missing and feared drowned. The survivors were handed over to the Red Cross in the Canaries. The accident is one of the deadliest recorded by the Spanish authorities since a wave of small vessels crammed with would-be immigrants began traveling from Africa to Europe in recent years.

2007-07-20.title: Nuclear Plant Logs 3rd Radiation Leak
2007-07-20.abstract: Tokyo Electric Power said that it had found tiny amounts of radioactive material in an exhaust filter at the plant, which was shut down Monday.

2007-07-20.title: Human Rat Trap Knows His Enemy. They’re Winning.
2007-07-20.abstract: An obsessive, fearsome rodent assassin in Mumbai, India, is a dying breed in a city whose dreams of being rat-free recede year by year.

2007-07-21.title: Chief Justice Is Reinstated in Pakistan
2007-07-21.abstract: A ruling by Pakistan's Supreme Court posed a serious challenge to President Pervez Musharraf, who had suspended the chief justice.

2007-07-21.title: Preparing for a Tsunami in Padang, Indonesia
2007-07-21.abstract: A private group is working to educate the public in one town in Indonesia on how to respond in the event of a tsunami or earthquake.

2007-07-21.title: Bolivia: Proposed Capital Move Draws Protest
2007-07-21.abstract: A polemical proposal to transfer the seat of government to Sucre, a genteel city of whitewashed buildings in central Bolivia that lost that distinction in a brief civil war at the end of the 19th century, drew hundreds of thousands of demonstrators into the streets of high-altitude La Paz, where the president and legislature are based. Many supporters of President Evo Morales who are from the Western highlands oppose the idea, seeing it as an effort by relatively prosperous lowland states in the east to weaken the president’s authority. The proposal has been put forward by delegates working to rewrite the Constitution. Proponents of the change view moving the seat of government to Sucre, already home to the Supreme Court and considered the country’s judicial capital, as a way to draw government spending away from La Paz in the west into Bolivia’s central regions.

2007-07-21.title: Indonesian Town Begins Preparation for the Next Tsunami
2007-07-21.abstract: Scientists say that Padang, Indonesia, may well be hit by a tsunami within the next 30 years, and a private group is working to educate the public on how to respond.

2007-07-21.title: Out of Britain, Firebrand Sheik Takes New Tone in Lebanon
2007-07-21.abstract: A cleric says he is pushing for a more peaceful way to carry out his vision.

2007-07-22.title: Turkey at a Crossroads
2007-07-22.abstract: The next chapter of Turkish history is unfolding with a power struggle between an old secular elite and a new, religious class.

2007-07-22.title: New Leaders Say Pensive French Think Too Much
2007-07-22.abstract: In the country of the Enlightenment, President Nicolas Sarkozy’s government advocates hard work, not musing.

2007-07-22.title: Turkish Group Wields Wit as Tool for Political Change
2007-07-22.abstract: Using both humor and conviction, a group called the Young Civilians is one of several starting to question Turkey’s established hierarchy.

2007-07-22.title: Israeli Text for Arabs Refers to 1948 ‘Catastrophe’
2007-07-22.abstract: A book for use in Arab schools describes Israel’s war of independence as a “catastrophe” for the Arab population.

2007-07-22.title: Fatah Militants Lay Down Arms to Bolster Abbas
2007-07-22.abstract: For both Israel and the Aksa Martyrs Brigades in the West Bank, the emerging cease-fire is an experiment.

2007-07-23.title: Bomb by Bomb, Japan Sheds Military Restraints
2007-07-23.abstract: Japan is blurring the line between defensive and offensive military power in spite of its pacifist Constitution, rattling nerves in northeast Asia.

2007-07-23.title: Flooding in Britain, Texas and China
2007-07-23.abstract: Floods caused misery, cut essential services and forced evacuations in widely spaced parts of the globe today.

2007-07-23.title: Political Clashes Shake Venezuela’s Strained Oil Industry
2007-07-23.abstract: Internal dissent is shaking Venezuela’s state-run oil company, threatening its ability to  finance Hugo Chávez’s ambitious social welfare programs.

2007-07-23.title: In Arabic Textbook, Israel Calls ’48 War Catastrophe for Arabs
2007-07-23.abstract: A book for use in Arab schools describes Israel’s war of independence as a “catastrophe” for the Arab population.

2007-07-23.title: Taliban Extend Deadline for Captives
2007-07-23.abstract: The Taliban had threatened to kill 23 South Korean hostages unless an equal number of Taliban fighters were released.

2007-07-24.title: Swaths of Britain Are Inundated
2007-07-24.abstract: With the Severn, Thames, and other rivers already at full capacity, and more rain on the way, severe flood warnings remained in effect for many parts of the country.

2007-07-24.title: Hard-Line Gain in Turkish Vote Poses Challenge to Governing Party
2007-07-24.abstract: On the margins, more hard-line sentiments surfaced, posing a potential obstacle to this country’s progress.

2007-07-24.title: Global Poll Finds Optimism in Developing World
2007-07-24.abstract: A new global opinion poll finds that people in developing countries are broadly more satisfied with their lives than five years ago.

2007-07-24.title: Car Bombs Kill 12 People in One of Baghdad’s Safer Neighborhoods
2007-07-24.abstract: Car bombs ripped through Karrada, a largely Shiite neighborhood that is normally one of Baghdad’s least dangerous areas.

2007-07-24.title: Confusion Pervades Afghanistan Hostage Ordeals
2007-07-24.abstract: Little seems certain except that in traditional Afghan fashion, tribal elders have become involved as go-betweens in trying to broker a solution.

2007-07-25.title: Ousted Shanghai Party Boss Is Stripped of Parliament Post
2007-07-25.abstract: The action, essentially stripping Chen Liangyu  of  legal immunity, signals that he is likely to face criminal charges soon.

2007-07-25.title: Zimbabwe: Mugabe to Pursue Nationalization
2007-07-25.abstract: President Robert G. Mugabe delivered a combative speech at the opening session of Parliament, accusing Western nations of redoubling their efforts to topple him from power and calling Zimbabwe’s economic collapse part of a plot to destabilize his 27-year rule. Despite inflation that is estimated to exceed 10,000 percent, Mr. Mugabe called price increases in stores “inexplicable” and said he was determined to continue a program to force retailers to lower the prices of their goods. He also proposed legislation that would nationalize mines and require all companies to be Zimbabwean-controlled. Mr. Mugabe arrived for his speech in a Rolls-Royce convertible.

2007-07-25.title: Afghanistan: Taliban Still Holding Koreans
2007-07-25.abstract: Negotiations over the fate of 23 South Korean hostages continued, Afghan officials and a man claiming to be a spokesman for the Taliban said. The insurgents had threatened to kill the Koreans unless demands were met for a release of Taliban prisoners and the removal of 210 South Korean soldiers in Afghanistan. Two Taliban deadlines have come and gone. Tribal elders in Ghazni Province, where the Koreans were kidnapped, are serving as intermediaries.

2007-07-25.title: Sudan: Milder U.N. Draft Resolution on Darfur Force
2007-07-25.abstract: Western sponsors of a draft Security Council resolution that would create a 26,000-member joint United Nations-African Union peacekeeping force for the Darfur region toned it down to meet African objections by dropping the threat of sanctions on Sudan for non-compliance. Abdalmahmood Abdalhaleem Mohamad, Sudan’s ambassador, said the revised text was still not acceptable. Andrew S. Natsios, the United States’ special envoy on Darfur, said the Sudanese government ”should not have veto power over what happens.”.

2007-07-25.title: A Dark, Gloomy Summer in England Turns Menacing
2007-07-25.abstract: This summer has brought unusually abysmal weather to Britain. An average of 10 inches of rain in June and July is just shy of the record, set in 1768.

2007-07-26.title: 3 Freed Medical Workers Describe Ordeal of Captivity in Libya
2007-07-26.abstract: Two Bulgarian nurses and a Palestinian doctor that they wanted to move on with their lives but were still too shaken after more than eight years in a Libyan prison.

2007-07-26.title: Car Bomb Kills 25 in Shiite Area of Baghdad
2007-07-26.abstract: A car bomb killed 25 people in a Shiite area of Baghdad during the evening rush hour on Thursday, wounding dozens of shopper and destroying stores.

2007-07-26.title: Aid From China Builds an Ally in East Timor
2007-07-26.abstract: East Timor, a country with few natural resources and only marginal geographical advantages, shows signs of a growing Chinese presence as China courts its neighbors.

2007-07-26.title: House Resolution Rejects Permanent Bases in Iraq
2007-07-26.abstract: The House voted overwhelmingly to bar permanent U.S. military installations in Iraq as lawmakers readied for yet another clash over a demand to withdraw troops.

2007-07-26.title: Japan’s Leader Attacked by His Own Party
2007-07-26.abstract: With Shinzo Abe’s future as prime minister hanging by a thread, politicians from his own party are distancing themselves for their own survival.

2007-07-27.title: Saudis’ Role in Iraq Frustrates U.S. Officials
2007-07-27.abstract: Bush administration officials are expressing increasing anger at what they say has been Saudi Arabia’s counterproductive role in the Iraq war.

2007-07-27.title: In the Amazon: Conservation or Colonialism?
2007-07-27.abstract: A recent survey showed that a majority of Brazilians feel the country’s natural riches could provoke a foreign invasion and many distrusted environmental groups.

2007-07-27.title: Russia Ordered to Pay Relatives of 11 Chechens Killed by Troops
2007-07-27.abstract: The European Court of Human Rights issued an unusually harsh rebuke of the Russian government on Thursday.

2007-07-27.title: Abbas Aide Resigns in Wake of Fatah Rout in Gaza
2007-07-27.abstract: Muhammad Dahlan  was trusted by Israel and the United States and some saw him as a potential successor to the Palestinian president, Mahmoud Abbas.

2007-07-27.title: U.S. to Announce Nuclear Exception for India
2007-07-27.abstract: The plan would help India build a nuclear fuel repository and find alternative sources of nuclear fuel.

2007-07-28.title: From Jail, Colombian Warlord Ponders Long Years of Conflict
2007-07-28.abstract: The confessions of Salvatore Mancuso have fed the slow-burning scandal over revelations of ties between paramilitaries and a web of elite politicians in Colombia.

2007-07-28.title: British Report Criticizes U.S. Treatment of Terror Suspects
2007-07-28.abstract: The report sharply criticized the Bush administration’s practice of rendition, saying it presented “some ethical dilemmas” for the British.

2007-07-28.title: As U.S. Rebuilds, Iraq Won’t Act on Finished Work
2007-07-28.abstract: Iraq’s national government is refusing to take over thousands of reconstruction projects, a report shows.

2007-07-28.title: Russia: Blast on Nuclear Sub; No Leak
2007-07-28.abstract: A Russian nuclear submarine was damaged from an explosion caused by pressurized air while undergoing repairs at the Severodvinsk shipyard on the White Sea, the Russian news media reported. The authorities termed the accident minor and said no radiation had leaked. Russia has a history of accidents involving its nuclear submarine fleet, including an explosion onboard the Kursk in 2000 that sank the ship and killed 118 sailors.

2007-07-28.title: Japanese Agricultural Minister Accused of Overcharging Government for Private Office Costs
2007-07-28.abstract: Days before a parliamentary election that Prime Minister Shinzo Abe’s party is expected to lose, fresh revelations about his scandal-ridden agriculture minister emerged.

2007-07-29.title: Panama Weighs Debt and Threat of Noriega
2007-07-29.abstract: With the deposed dictator’s imprisonment ending soon, there are mixed feelings about his possible return.

2007-07-29.title: Libya Faults Bulgarian Pardon of Medical Workers
2007-07-29.abstract: Libya’s government condemned Bulgaria’s release of medical workers who had been turned over earlier in the week, calling it a betrayal.

2007-07-29.title: Japan’s Prime Minister Tries to Avoid Big Loss
2007-07-29.abstract: The news media were predicting a loss for Prime Minister Shinzo Abe’s Liberal Democratic Party in  Sunday’s elections for Japan’s upper house of Parliament.

2007-07-29.title: On Eve of Asian Cup Final, Iraq Is the Proud Underdog
2007-07-29.abstract: Having overcome countless obstacles, the Iraqi national soccer team will face three-time champion Saudi Arabia in Sunday’s Asian Cup final.

2007-07-29.title: China Moves to Change Damaged Global Image
2007-07-29.abstract: China is  cracking down on pirated goods and has vowed to hold producers responsible for unsafe products.

2007-07-30.title: Odile Crick, Who Drew Iconic Double Helix, Dies at 86
2007-07-30.abstract: Mrs. Crick’s  original illustration of the structure of DNA, discovered by her husband, Francis H. C. Crick and James D. Watson, became a symbol of modern molecular biology.

2007-07-30.title: A Super-Size Troupe Leaps From Ridiculous to Sublime
2007-07-30.abstract: A dance company in Cuba is breaking stereotypes of dance, redefining the aesthetics of beauty and raising the self-esteem of heavyset people.

2007-07-30.title: Governing Party in Japan Suffers Election Defeat
2007-07-30.abstract: The opposition party seized control of the upper house, but Prime Minister Shinzo Abe said he would not step down.

2007-07-30.title: British Prime Minister Holds Talks With Bush
2007-07-30.abstract: Gordon Brown’s visit to Camp David is being closely watched for signs of how his relationship with President Bush will differ from that of his predecessor, Tony Blair.

2007-07-30.title: Report Finds Dire Humanitarian Crisis in Iraq
2007-07-30.abstract: As many as four million Iraqis need immediate help getting food, a report from Oxfam International warns.

2007-07-31.title: Corn That Never Came
2007-07-31.abstract: Farmers in northwestern Kenya are caught up in American rules that benefit domestic agribusiness.

2007-07-31.title: South Africa: Fires Kill at Least 19
2007-07-31.abstract: Wildfires racing across vast grasslands claimed at least 19 lives over the weekend, including those of firefighters, the government said. Thirteen deaths were in the eastern province of KwaZulu-Natal. In northwestern Mpumalanga, the wind-whipped fires outraced fleeing vehicles, and five firefighters died when theirs was overtaken. The fires destroyed tree farms and sawmills, and killed large numbers of animals. Most were reported under control yesterday.

2007-07-31.title: A New Country’s Tough Non-Elective: Portuguese 101
2007-07-31.abstract: Portuguese, the newly designated language of courts, schools and the government in East Timor, is a language that most people in the country cannot speak.

2007-07-31.title: Bush and Brown Are Allies if Not Buddies
2007-07-31.abstract: Britain’s prime minister, Gordon Brown, displayed what can best be described as a resounding — if dispassionate — show of like-minded camaraderie with President Bush.

2007-07-31.title: Brazil, Alarmed, Reconsiders Policy on Climate Change
2007-07-31.abstract: Brazilian policy makers have begun showing signs of new flexibility in the tangled, politically volatile international negotiations to limit human-caused global warming.
